title: Diphasiastrum angustiramosum
taxonomy: Diphasiastrum angustiramosum is a species of plant in the genus Diphasiastrum and is classified as a vascular plant. It belongs to the division Lycopodiophyta, which includes all club mosses. This classification is based on the plant's unique characteristics, such as its simple leaves and vascular tissue. Within the division, it is further classified as a member of the order Lycopodiales. Its classification is part of the plant kingdom's hierarchical system, which includes various levels of organization from kingdom to species.

intro: Diphasiastrum angustiramosum, also known as the narrow-leaved forked spike-moss, is a type of epiphytic, non-vascular plant belonging to the family Lycopodiaceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Diphasiastrum angustiramosum include lycopene, lycopodine, and other alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures to take advantage of its therapeutic benefits, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
